response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong technical depth across 20 years of software development and has relevant DevOps/CI infrastructure experience, they critically lack the required people management background. The resume shows zero evidence of team leadership, direct reports, or people management responsibilities—failing the explicit minimum requirement of 2 years in team leadership. This is a people-focused manager role that needs an experienced manager with a track record of leading teams, setting performance expectations, and developing talent, not a senior individual contributor.
strengths: 20 years of software development experience with demonstrated expertise in system design, architecture, and building scalable systems at high-traffic companies like Dunnhumby
Direct experience with developer tools infrastructure including GitLab administration, CI/CD server setup, Docker deployment, and build automation at Altom Consulting
Proven ability to teach and mentor through workshops, technical talks, and creating educational content, showing communication skills and passion for knowledge-sharing
concerns: Zero people management experience—no mention of managing teams, conducting 1-on-1s, setting performance expectations, or having direct reports; fails the minimum 2-year people leadership requirement
Developer tools focus is limited and dated; only ~2 years (2016-2018) spent on infrastructure work while remainder of career focused on application and full-stack development across diverse domains
No evidence of setting organizational priorities, managing roadmaps, leading change initiatives, or demonstrating the people-leadership readiness expected for a mid-to-senior manager role

key_factors.factor: Technical depth and system design capability
